what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an attachment id to a target image URI and noting its type and creation time.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
target is 'image://design-assets/diagrams/image (14)', including spaces and parentheses.
standouts: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses: 'image (14)'.
Attachment is typed as images in the design-assets channel.
File is a bare metadata stub with only id, channel, type, target, and added fields.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured attachment metadata.
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme scoped to the channel path.
concerns: Target URI includes literal spaces and parentheses, which may require escaping in downstream resolution.
No title, caption, filename, or checksum is recorded, limiting discoverability and integrity checks.
notes: A minimal ledger stub that registers an image attachment reference for the design-assets channel.
questions: What physical file or blob does 'image://design-assets/diagrams/image (14)' resolve to?
Is 'image (14)' the actual stored filename or a display label?
Why is there no title, caption, or uploader field?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an attachment metadata record and contains exactly the fields needed to identify and locate an image attachment.
apparent_themes: Custom URI references for stored media
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped record that associates a design-assets channel attachment with its underlying image resource.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme and the use of a literal filename with spaces/parentheses as a structured target.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment identifier and a typed link between a channel attachment and a stored image asset, which a fused system could use as a reference node for media retrieval.
